title: kayea parviflora
intro: Kayea parviflora, also known as the 'small-flowered kayea', is a species of plant in the family Clusiaceae, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of kayenol, a unique compound found in the plant. The health benefits of Kayea parviflora include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues, making it a popular remedy in traditional medicine. Kayea parviflora is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for its medicinal uses, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
